Title: Pledging Another $400,000 to the Zig Software Foundation
Description:
My family is pledging another $400,0001 to the
Zig Software Foundation (ZSF). This brings
our total pledged support for ZSF to $700,000, after our
initial donation in 2024.

Title: Never Give Them Your Face
Description: They are not verifying your age. They are building a permanent identity checkpoint over public life. Here is why to refuse.

Title: GLM-5.2 vs Claude Opus | Tech Stackups
Description: Is the GLM-5.2 hype real or benchmaxxed? We ran it head-to-head against Claude Opus, building a 3D platformer in raw WebGL from scratch.

Title: Desktop apps
Description: Build self-contained desktop applications from a Deno project, with framework auto-detection, hot reload, native windowing, auto-update, and cross-platform distribution.

Title: Everything you say CAN and WILL be used against you
Description:
- "If you talk to me, I'll punch you in the face, are you ok with talking with me?"
- "Nods in agreement."
- "Proceeds to punch the man in the face."

Title: Fully Open Foundation Model for Sovereign AI
Description: Fully Open Foundation Model for Sovereign AI

Title: Happy Father's Day.
Description: An Ode to my father. The man who shaped me into who I am today. I hope I can one day fill half his shoes. For now, I'll remember him for the giant he was.

Title: Why I Moved from Arch to Boring Debian Testing | WhileForLoop
Description: The recent AUR incident did not make Arch unusable, but it changed how I think about trust, package sources, and the amount of maintenance I want from my desktop.

Title: Looping Casino
Description: Autonomous coding loops excel at search, not judgment. They can optimize toward a goal, but they can’t reliably tell when the goal is wrong. Engineering is still more than iteration speed.

Title: When Rails Engines Are the Wrong Tool
Description: Rails engines aren’t free. When adding engine boundaries is over-engineering — the performance, boot-time and memory costs — and how to tell when a boundary actually earns its keep.

Title: Did my old job only exist because of fraud? – It's not about code
Description: Early in my software engineering career, the UK-based startup I worked at, GenieDB, was taken over by a US Venture Capital fund, Frost VP, owned by Stuart Frost.  I was functionally the only piece that came to the US. The code was rebuilt, the rest of the team eventually rotated out, even the core strategy was replaced. But I was early in my career and excited to be working in the VC tech-startup world. I ended up making my life in the US, so this was a pretty pivotal phase in my life.

Title: A Small Fintech Challenge, and Why I Let Postgres Hold the Money
Description: I took a small fintech-style coding challenge — a marketplace where players buy and auction items with in-game gold — and treated it as one question: how do you keep money correct when many people act at once? The answer wasn't a clever lock in my code. It was leaning on what Postgres already guarantees.

Title: I want to spend more time on the internet
The Wrong Abstraction — Sandi Metz
Description: I've been thinking about the consequences of the "wrong abstraction."  My RailsConf 2014 "all the little things" talk included a section where I asserted:  > duplication is far cheaper than the wrong abstraction  And in the summary, I went on to advise:  >

Title: Thinking at the edge
Description: Getting locked out of a BitLocker laptop with a frozen keyboard brought back a feeling I hadn't had since LLMs took over my workflow: the satisfaction of thinking at the edge of a hard problem.

Title: Real-time dreamy Cloudscapes with Volumetric Raymarching - The Blog of Maxime Heckel
Description: This article is a deep dive into my experimentations with Volumetric rendering and how to leverage it to render beautiful raymarched cloudscapes in React Three Fiber and WebGL. In it, I walk you through everything from the basics of Volumetric Raymarching to the techniques used in video games to render physically accurate clouds.

Title: Identity verification on Claude | Claude Help Center
Nix needs relocatable binaries
Description: This is my problem statement and proposal for a TacoSprint 2026 project 🏄.

Title: We built a trading bot in two hours. Now my wife and I trade together.
Description: We had two Robinhood accounts and no shared view of anything. Schwab has a free API most people don't know about. We migrated, built a bot in two hours, and it changed how we think about money together.

Title: Snowboard Kids 2 is Recompiled
Description: Snowboard Kids 2 has been recompiled for modern platforms, bringing widescreen, 60 FPS support, and modding to Snow Town.
